extract.name: Landing Pages
extract.tagline: One page. One offer. Designed to convert. Built in under 7 business days, or we refund the deposit.
extract.summary: A done-for-you landing page service offering three tiers (Standard, Extended, Full Site) that builds a single-offer, conversion-first page with researched copy, custom design, mobile-first sub-2-second load, SEO/schema, and a contact form. Pages ship in under 7 business days (2-3 weeks for full sites) with three revision rounds included.
extract.pricing: Standard: $350 one-time. Extended: $450 one-time. Full Site: starting at $800 (scales up to 6 to 10 pages on quote). Prices in USD. A deposit at booking secures the build slot. Balance on launch.
included: Standard ($350): Up to 7 sections, single offer
Standard ($350): Researched copy, your approval per section
Standard ($350): Custom design, no template feel
Standard ($350): Mobile-first, sub-2 second load
Standard ($350): SEO meta tags and JSON-LD schema
Standard ($350): Contact form into your inbox
Standard ($350): Three revision rounds included
Standard ($350): Built in under 7 business days
Extended ($450): All 8 sections, success flow included
Extended ($450): Video hero with poster fallback
Extended ($450): Scroll reveals and microinteractions
Extended ($450): Bilingual content if needed
Extended ($450): Multi-CTA strategy, primary plus secondary
Extended ($450): Advanced schema, OG image package
Extended ($450): Three revision rounds included
Extended ($450): Built in under 7 business days
Full Site ($800+): 3 to 5 pages, shared design system
Full Site ($800+): Home, About, Services, Contact, plus one custom
Full Site ($800+): Per-page SEO and schema
Full Site ($800+): Shared header, footer, and form
Full Site ($800+): Cross-page navigation tested
Full Site ($800+): Built in 2 to 3 weeks
Full Site ($800+): Three revision rounds included
Full Site ($800+): Scales up to 6 to 10 pages on quote
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): One offer, locked
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Researched copy
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Conversion-first layout
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Custom visuals
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Sub-2 second load
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): SEO and schema baked in
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Form to your inbox
Eight core deliverables (all tiers): Security and revisions
keyPoints: A landing page is not a website. It is one decision, asked one time
No menu of services, no scroll fatigue, no confusion
Headlines built from real customer language
Eight proven sections: hero, problem, value stack, proof, transformation, CTA
Mobile-first build with compressed assets; fast pages convert more
How it works Day 1: Intake and research (34-question brief, 30-minute market research)
How it works Day 2: Strategy and copy draft (section map, conversion hook, approval)
How it works Day 3: Design system (typography, color, layout, motion)
How it works Days 4-5: Build and assets (mobile-first coding, image sourcing, microinteractions)
How it works Day 6: QA and security pass (Playwright testing, form checks, security audit)
How it works Day 7: Launch (deploy to Vercel or host, source files, handoff docs)
CTA buttons: Book a discovery call, See all pricing, Start the Standard build, Start the Extended build, Get a full site quote, Book the discovery call
proof: Medical Aesthetics, Edmonton: Before, 800 visitors/month to a 5-page site with under 1% form conversion. After: 312% lift in booking form submissions, first 30 days
Home Services, Alberta: Before, 71% bounce rate, $84 cost per lead. After: 47% drop in cost per qualified lead
B2B SaaS, Remote: Before, long-form page with 7 CTAs and confused leads. After: 184% increase in demo bookings, quarter over quarter
faq: Q: What does the 7 business day promise actually cover? A: From the moment your intake form and deposit clear, you get the live page within 7 business days. If we miss that window for any reason on our side, the deposit comes back. Client-side delays on copy approval or asset delivery pause the clock and we will tell you when they do.
Q: Can you write the copy or do I need to provide it? (answer in collapsed accordion, not captured in static fetch)
Q: Do you build on WordPress, Wix, Squarespace, or something else? (answer in collapsed accordion, not captured in static fetch)
Q: How does the refund work if I am not happy? (answer in collapsed accordion, not captured in static fetch)
Q: Is hosting and the domain included? (answer in collapsed accordion, not captured in static fetch)
Q: What if I want a chat agent or voice agent on the page later? (answer in collapsed accordion, not captured in static fetch)
extract.capgRemoved: STRIPPED the following grant/funding content found on the page: (1) A header/banner line: "The Canada-Alberta Job Grant covers up to 66 percent of AI training, with up to $10,000 per trainee per year." (2) A site-level positioning line: "AI workforce training and digital services for Alberta operators. CAJG-eligible programs and a digital stack tuned for revenue." (3) An entire FAQ question "Will the Canada-Alberta Job Grant cover this?" was removed from the faq array (its accordion answer was not rendered anyway). Removed all CAJG / Canada-Alberta Job Grant / "66 percent" / "$10,000 per trainee per year" / grant-eligible / "Alberta operators" funding framing. The underlying service and its real prices ($350 / $450 / $800+ one-time, deposit at booking) are kept intact.

copy.seoDescription: We build websites and the AI behind them. One premium build or a monthly plan with hosting, updates, and support. Book a call.

sections.headline: A website that sells, built fast
sections.subhead: Not a brochure. A page that turns visitors into booked calls.
sections.body: We design it, build it, and wire in the AI that follows up. Live in days, not months.

sections.eyebrow: THE COST OF A BAD SITE
sections.headline: A dead site loses leads daily
sections.subhead: Every problem below is a customer who booked with someone else.
bullets: Loads slow, so people bounce before they read
Looks dated, so they doubt you before they call
No clear next step, so nobody takes action
Goes quiet after launch, then breaks
sections.body: Most sites are pretty and silent. Yours should book the call while you sleep.

sections.headline: Buy it once, or pay monthly
sections.subhead: Same quality. Pick the model that fits your cash flow.
sections.body: One-shot is a premium build you own outright. Website-as-a-Service spreads the cost into a low monthly fee with everything handled.
bullets: One-shot build: custom design, you own it, single upfront price
One-shot fit: you have budget now and want full ownership
Website-as-a-Service: low monthly fee, no big upfront, cancel anytime
WaaS fit: you want it live fast with zero maintenance headaches
Both ship fast and both include the AI follow-up layer
sections.flags: two_modes is a side-by-side comparison block. Render One-shot and WaaS as two columns.

sections.headline: One monthly fee covers everything
sections.subhead: No surprise invoices. No chasing a developer who vanished.
bullets: Custom design built around your offer
Hosting and your domain, set up and managed
Updates and content changes when you need them
Maintenance, security, and uptime handled for you
Real support after launch, cancel anytime
sections.body: We stay on after go-live. The site keeps working and so do we.

sections.headline: Fast, mobile, and built to sell
sections.subhead: Every page is engineered for the click, not just the look.
bullets: Loads in under two seconds, so visitors stay
Built around one clear action per page
Flawless on phones, where most traffic lives
SEO done right, so the right people find you

sections.headline: Numbers our clients actually see
sections.subhead: Proof, not promises. Swap these for your live figures.
bullets: [PLACEHOLDER: X% more booked calls in the first 30 days]
[PLACEHOLDER: site live in X days from kickoff]
[PLACEHOLDER: X leads captured per month, on autopilot]
[PLACEHOLDER: client quote, full name, business]
sections.flags: All four bullets are placeholders. Replace with real metrics and a named testimonial before launch.

sections.headline: Clear pricing, no surprises
sections.subhead: Pick a one-time build or a monthly plan. Full breakdown on the pricing page.
sections.body: One-shot builds start at [REVIEW: $X]. Website-as-a-Service starts at [REVIEW: $X per month].
sections.flags: Both prices are [REVIEW]. CTA points to /pricing. Confirm starting numbers before publishing.

sections.headline: Answers before you ask
bullets: How fast can it go live? Most builds ship in days, not weeks. [PLACEHOLDER: typical timeline]
Can I cancel the monthly plan? Yes. Website-as-a-Service is cancel anytime, no lock-in.
Do I own the site? One-shot builds are yours outright. WaaS stays managed by us while you subscribe.
What about the AI part? Every site can include a WhatsApp agent that answers and books leads 24/7.
sections.flags: Timeline answer has a [PLACEHOLDER]. Confirm exact turnaround before publishing.

sections.headline: Get a site that books the call
sections.subhead: Tell us your offer. We will show you exactly what we would build.
